summary: OpenNori Architecture Profile/Baseline now supports concrete technical baselines for both built-in and project profiles. The built-in TypeScript agent-state CLI profile includes runtime topology, source-of-truth, module boundaries, contract surfaces, data flows, dependency decisions, reference mappings, and verification. OpenNori self check/status are architecture-valid, and Agent Workbench check/status validates a non-OpenNori project baseline with populated technical section counts.

sources.label: ac-a-3-technical-baseline-sections
sources.summary: Architecture Baseline alignment context. This is not Product AC evidence by itself.

reviewability: Rerun npm run check, node ./bin/opennori.js check --root . --json, node ./bin/opennori.js status --root . --json, and node ./bin/opennori.js check/status --root /Users/jarl/code/jarlone/agent-workbench --json; inspect schemas/architecture-baseline.schema.json, src/architecture/profile.ts, src/architecture/baseline.ts, packaged architecture Skills, .opennori/architecture/baseline.md, and AW .opennori/architecture/baseline.md.
limitations: This verifies objective structure, generated baseline state, Skill guidance, and one AW project baseline. It intentionally does not make CLI judge subjective architecture quality; agents and users still review whether a baseline is specific and appropriate before confirmation.
